conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the Child Benefit eligibility rules. To help determine if you're eligible, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I've got one child - his name's Alex, and he's 8 years old. He lives at home with me here in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Are you or your partner currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else claimed Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex at the moment. I haven't claimed it before either - that's why I'm looking into whether I'm eligible now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Now I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do - I'm in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or does he live with you at home full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex is actually fostered - I'm fostering him. He lives with me at home. I don't get anything from the council to foster him though, no maintenance payments or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important information. Just to clarify:

Is the local council paying anything at all towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs while you're fostering him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing at all. The council isn't paying me anything for his accommodation or maintenance. I cover all his costs myself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You are fostering Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you at home in Bristol, UK
- The local council is not paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance
- Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the official Child Benefit rules, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for a foster child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. Since the council is not paying you anything for Alex's care, you meet this requirement.

You also meet the other key criteria:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for him (he lives with you)
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

As a foster carer who is not receiving any payments from the local council for Alex's accommodation or maintenance, you qualify for Child Benefit. Since Alex is 8 years old, lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming for him, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Please note: if either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16) and lives with the claimant in Bristol, UK. The claimant is fostering Alex and the local council is not paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for Alex, Alex lives with them, they live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering 8-year-old Alex who lives with them in Bristol, UK. The local council does not pay anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Under UK Child Benefit rules, foster carers are eligible for Child Benefit as long as the local council is not paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for Alex, Alex lives with them, they live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The local council does not pay anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Under UK Child Benefit rules, foster carers are eligible for Child Benefit as long as the local council is not paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
